steps.title: Similar triangles have equal corresponding angles
steps.content:
When two triangles are similar, their corresponding angles are equal, regardless of the side length scale factor. Since triangle ABC ~ triangle XYZ with A↔X, B↔Y, and C↔Z:
∠Z = ∠C
To find ∠Z, we must first find ∠C in triangle ABC.

steps.title: Apply the angle sum property of triangles
steps.content:
Every triangle's interior angles sum to 180°. For triangle ABC:
$$∠A + ∠B + ∠C = 180°$$
Substitute the given values:
$$44° + 24° + ∠C = 180°$$
$$∠C = 112°$$
Since ∠Z = ∠C, the measure of angle Z is 112°.
